description: In 2002, Hewlett-Packard CEO Carly Fiorina pushed for a controversial merger with Compaq, leading to a bitter proxy battle with Walter Hewlett. The merger was eventually approved by a razor-thin margin after a intense shareholder campaign regarding the company's future direction.

key_events.description: Walter Hewlett announces formal opposition to the merger.
Institutional Shareholder Services (ISS) recommends voting against the merger.
HP management initiates aggressive lobbying campaign with major institutional investors.
The final shareholder vote concludes with a narrow victory for the pro-merger board.

calibration_notes: Useful for calibrating models on high-stakes corporate proxy battles where institutional sentiment shifts late.
